messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Customizations dialog: plugins tab only shows martketplace plugins
ISSUE BODY:  - start without any plugins installed
 - got to the plugns tab on the customization diallog
 - click on 'Browse Marketplace`: this will show a list of plugins to install
 - now click 'Install Plugin from Source`, enter `https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-team-kit` and pick a plugin

:bug: 
- No information that the plugin has been installed
- The overview did not increase the counter: should show that 1 plugin is installed
- the plugin page still shows the marketplace plugins. I don't find a way to go back to the view of installed plugins


--- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) ---
--- File: openproject_full.md ---
### Plugin  
In OpenProject, you can choose from several plugins or [integrations](#integration), or add your own plugins to the Community. In contrast to an integration, a plugin is installed on top of / as part of the OpenProject installation. As an open source software, OpenProject is open to Community-created plugins. Please note that we do not guarantee error-free and seamless use of those plugins.  
Your activated plugins are listed together with your [modules](#module) in your instance under → Administration → Plugins.  
**More information on plugins in OpenProject**  
- [See all available plugins and integrations for OpenProject](../system-admin-guide/integrations/)
- [Read how to create an OpenProject plugin](../development/create-openproject-plugin/)

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## Marketplace & Installation  
### IPluginMarketplaceService  
Manages the catalog of available and installed plugins:  
- **Fetch** — reads `chat.pluginMarketplaces` config (GitHub shorthand, Git URLs, or file URIs), fetches `marketplace.json` from each, and returns parsed `IMarketplacePlugin` entries.
- **Installed storage** — persists installed plugins in application-scoped storage (`chat.plugins.installed.v1`). Each entry tracks `{ pluginUri, plugin, enabled }`.
- **Trust** — marketplace canonical IDs must be explicitly trusted before install proceeds (`chat.plugins.trustedMarketplaces.v1`).
- **Auto-update** — checks for upstream changes approximately every 24 hours when `extensions.autoUpdate` is enabled; sets `hasUpdatesAvailable` observable.
- **GitHub caching** — caches raw GitHub API responses with an 8-hour TTL to avoid repeated fetches.

--- File: openproject_full.md ---
# Integrations and Community plugins  
There are various integrations and Community plugins out there. Please [contact us](https://www.openproject.org/contact/) if you want to have your plugin to be added to this list.  
If you have previously worked with other tools and want to switch or need an integration to OpenProject, there is a way to do so for some applications.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## Integration Points  
- **Chat prompts** — plugin hooks are contributed to the prompt system via the hook infrastructure.
- **MCP servers** — plugins can define MCP server configurations (stdio or SSE) that are registered with the MCP platform.
- **AI Customization UI** — the AI customization views aggregate plugin stats alongside MCP servers, prompts, and other customization surfaces.
- **Extension API** — the plugin system is internal; there is no public `vscode` API surface for third-party access.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### IAgentPluginDiscovery  
Strategy for finding plugins from a particular source:  
``` plugins – IObservable<readonly IAgentPlugin[] | undefined> start(enablementModel) – Begin watching the source
```  
Discoveries are registered into the global `agentPluginDiscoveryRegistry` singleton with a priority and instantiated by `AgentPluginService` on startup. A discovery reports `undefined` until its first scan completes; the service waits until every discovery has an initial result before exposing plugins.

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Customizations dialog: Support installing plugins from GitHub/source URLs",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "plugin-system"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a power user, I want to install plugins from custom source URLs (like GitHub repositories) so that I can extend OpenProject's functionality beyond the official marketplace.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given no plugins are installed, When I click 'Install Plugin from Source' and enter a valid GitHub URL, Then the plugin is successfully fetched and displayed in the list",
    "Given a plugin has been installed from source URL, When I view the Customizations dialog overview, Then the installed plugins counter increments by 1",
    "Given a custom source plugin is installed, When I click on it in the marketplace tab, Then the plugin page shows installation details and provides a 'Go to Installed Plugins' navigation option",
    "Given a plugin was successfully installed from source, When I complete the installation flow, Then a success confirmation message is displayed to the user",
    "Given multiple plugins are installed including custom sources, When I navigate between marketplace and installed views, Then both lists correctly display all installed plugins"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Implement plugin discovery for GitHub source URLs in IAgentPluginDiscovery strategy",
      "Add support for parsing GitHub repository URLs to extract plugin metadata",
      "Update IPluginMarketplaceService to handle custom source URL installations",
      "Ensure installed plugins from non-marketplace sources are persisted in chat.plugins.installed.v1 storage",
      "Add navigation endpoint/route to view all installed plugins regardless of source"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Update Customizations dialog to show 'Install Plugin from Source' input field with validation",
      "Display plugin details fetched from custom sources in the marketplace tab",
      "Add success confirmation modal/message after installing from source URL",
      "Implement navigation link/button on plugin detail page to return to installed plugins view",
      "Update installed plugins counter display in overview section"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test GitHub URL parsing and validation for plugin sources",
      "Verify plugin installation success state is properly communicated to user",
      "Confirm installed plugins counter updates correctly after source installation",
      "Validate navigation between marketplace tab and installed plugins view works bidirectionally"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Backend: Plugin discovery supports GitHub URLs with proper metadata extraction",
    "Backend: Installed plugins from custom sources persist in storage",
    "Frontend: User can install plugins from source URL and receives confirmation",
    "Frontend: Installed plugins counter reflects all installed plugins including custom sources",
    "Frontend: Navigation between marketplace and installed views is functional",
    "QA: All acceptance criteria verified with test cases"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What is the expected behavior when a GitHub URL returns an invalid or non-plugin repository?",
    "Should custom source plugins be automatically enabled after installation, or require manual enablement?",
    "Are there any security/trust requirements for validating GitHub URLs before installation?",
    "Is there existing documentation on how to create OpenProject plugins that we should reference?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "GitHub repository URLs follow standard format: https://github.com/owner/repo",
    "Plugin metadata can be extracted from the repository's README or a marketplace.json file",
    "The existing plugin discovery infrastructure can be extended to support custom sources",
    "Users will provide valid GitHub URLs that contain actual plugins"
  ],
  "story_splits": [
    {
      "name": "Plugin Source Discovery & Installation",
      "description": "Backend implementation of GitHub URL parsing and plugin fetching from custom sources"
    },
    {
      "name": "UI for Custom Plugin Installation",
      "description": "Frontend implementation of source URL input, validation, and installation flow"
    },
    {
      "name": "Installed Plugins Navigation & Display",
      "description": "Frontend navigation to view all installed plugins regardless of source type"
    }
  ],
  "sprint_allocation": [
    {"story_id": "CUSTOM-101", "sprint": 42, "priority": "high"},
    {"story_id": "CUSTOM-102", "sprint": 43, "priority": "medium"}
  ],
  "context_sources": ["openproject_full.md", "vscode_ai_docs_full.md"]
}
```
